attributes.description: Alerts when Memcached CPU usage exceeds 0.8 cores. Memcached is typically CPU-light; high CPU indicates heavy workload or pathological access patterns.
attributes.name: [Memcached OTel] High CPU usage

investigation_guide.blob:
## Memcached high CPU usage

### What fired
CPU usage rate exceeded 0.8 cores during the window.

### Why it matters
Because of its single-threaded command processing, Memcached's CPU usage tracks linearly with command rate. High CPU is unusual and often indicates very large values, heavy traffic, or pathological access patterns.

### Triage
1. Correlate with command throughput.
2. Check item sizes and large-value usage.

### Remediation
- Scale horizontally.
- Reduce value size or client-side work-split.

### Tuning
- 0.8 cores for a busy cache; tune to your thread count (`-t`).

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-memcachedreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE memcached.cpu.usage IS NOT NULL
// CPU usage is cumulative seconds (system + user) — RATE gives cores in use
| STATS cpu_cores = SUM(RATE(memcached.cpu.usage))
  BY host.name, COALESCE(service.instance.id, host.name)
// Alert when CPU exceeds 0.8 cores — memcached is typically CPU-light
| WHERE cpu_cores > 0.8
| SORT cpu_cores DESC
| LIMIT 20
